At New Ivory Dental & Implant Clinic, ICON® infiltration is delivered using a microscope-assisted approach in selected cases, allowing enhanced precision, control, and finishing—particularly in high-aesthetic areas. What Is ICON® Resin Infiltration. ICON® infiltration is designed for early enamel lesions where the tooth surface remains intact.

A specialised resin is infiltrated into the porous enamel, reinforcing weakened tooth structure and reducing the visibility of white spots without removing healthy enamel. When performed using microscope-assisted dentistry, the procedure allows greater visual accuracy during lesion management, resin infiltration, and polishing.

Using a microscope-assisted technique allows careful control during each step, particularly in visible areas where accuracy and detail are essential. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] Microscope-Assisted, Minimally Invasive Dentistry at New Ivory ICON® infiltration reflects New Ivory’s commitment to minimally invasive and biomimetic dentistry. Treatment is focused on early intervention, preservation of natural tooth structure, and long-term oral health.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=16161 img_size=full css=][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=16162 img_size=full css=][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] Conditions ICON® Can Treat ICON infiltration may be suitable for: White spot lesions after orthodontic treatment Early enamel demineralisation Initial (non-cavitated) tooth decay Mild fluorosis and chalky enamel patches Suitability is determined through a clinical examination.
Where clinically appropriate, ICON treatment is performed using microscope-assisted dentistry, supporting precision, conservative care, and predictable outcomes.

ICON vs Traditional Fillings Unlike traditional fillings, ICON® infiltration does not involve drilling or removal of healthy tooth tissue. It is a conservative alternative for early lesions, focused on preservation rather than replacement. At New Ivory, a microscope-assisted ICON protocol may be used in appropriate cases to enhance precision and support optimal aesthetic outcomes.

Dental Clinic in Dubai

--- ICON Infiltration ---
[vc_section][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] ICON Infiltration in Dubai – White Spot Removal Without Drilling ICON® resin infiltration is a modern, micro-invasive treatment used to manage white spot lesions and early enamel decay without drilling or injections. It works by sealing porous enamel from within, helping stabilise early lesions and improve the appearance of affected teeth.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] How ICON® Infiltration Works ICON® infiltration is typically completed in a single visit. The affected enamel is gently prepared, followed by controlled resin infiltration into the lesion. The resin is then cured and polished.

He is trained in ICON® resin infiltration and contemporary enamel preservation techniques, and incorporates microscope-assisted dentistry in selected cases to support precision and detail in high-aesthetic, conservative treatments. As a biomimetic practice, our goal is to help teeth repair themselves whenever possible — preserving natural tooth structure and avoiding unnecessary invasive procedures. If you’re looking for a drill-free cavity treatment in Dubai, a non-invasive cavity treatment in the UAE, or a modern way to treat white spot lesions, Curodont offers one of the most advanced, tooth-preserving options available. What Is Curodont.
We use Near-Infrared Imaging (NIRI) — a radiation-free technology that allows us to detect demineralisation far earlier than traditional X-rays.

[/vc_toggle][vc_toggle title=Is it considered a biomimetic caries treatment. css=]100%.

Curodont Repair is an innovative biomimetic therapy designed for early enamel caries and white spot lesions. It uses a self-assembling peptide (P11-4) together with fluoride to guide minerals deep into weakened enamel, helping the tooth regenerate and re-harden from within. This makes Curodont one of the most scientifically validated and clinically effective biomimetic caries treatments available today.

It regenerates enamel through natural mineralisation — the core principle of biomimetic dentistry. [/vc_toggle][vc_toggle title=Is it effective for white spots after braces. css=]Yes. --- CURODONT DUBAI ---
[vc_section][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] CURODONT DUBAI – A DRILL-FREE, NON-INVASIVE CAVITY TREATMENT AT NEW IVORY At New Ivory Dental & Implant Clinic in Dubai, we use Curodont as a breakthrough solution for treating early cavities without drilling, needles, or fillings.

Benefits of Curodont for Patients Completely drill-free No anesthesia required Pain-free and comfortable Regenerates natural enamel biomimetically Ideal for early decay between teeth No staining or discoloration Quick, simple appointment Perfect for children and anxious adults [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text css=] Who Is a Good Candidate for Curodont.

Why NIRI is a major upgrade in early cavity detection Zero radiation – completely safe for all ages Clearly shows early enamel breakdown that X-rays often miss Ideal for spotting white spot lesions before cavitation Helps us determine whether lesions are active or stable Perfectly complements Curodont: treat at the reversible stage By seeing changes in enamel in real time, we can intervene early — often before a patient feels any symptoms — and use Curodont to help the tooth naturally remineralise without drilling.
This precise, conservative workflow prevents overtreatment and dramatically increases the success of non-invasive care.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] Why Curodont Is Becoming the Leading Non-Invasive Cavity Treatment in the UAE Unlike traditional drilling and filling, Curodont: Preserves almost all natural enamel Regenerates tooth structure instead of removing it Requires no anesthesia Does not stain teeth (unlike silver diamine fluoride) Works exceptionally well for early lesions Fits perfectly into the philosophy of biomimetic dentistry For patients wanting conservative, gentle care, Curodont is one of the most advanced minimally invasive treatments in Dubai.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] What to Expect at New Ivory 1. Comprehensive assessment We examine your teeth with magnification, digital X-rays, and NIRI to determine if the lesion is early, active and suitable for Curodont. 2.

No needles. No pain. Self-assembling peptide activation Inside the lesion, the peptide forms a scaffold that attracts calcium, phosphate and fluoride from saliva. Deep guided enamel regeneration Over the following weeks, the enamel rehardens from within, arresting decay and strengthening the tooth.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] How Curodont Works (Simple Explanation) Early detection Using magnification, X-rays and NIRI, we identify lesions early — when they’re still reversible. Tooth preparation The surface is cleaned and conditioned to allow Curodont to diffuse effectively. Application A small amount of Curodont is brushed or sponged onto the affected area. No drilling.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] How NIRI and Curodont work together We detect early lesions using NIRI, including those between teeth. We verify lesion activity, ensuring Curodont is appropriate. We apply Curodont directly to the weakened enamel, achieving deeper penetration. We monitor remineralisation with follow-up NIRI scans, avoiding unnecessary radiation.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=16163 img_size=full css=][/vc_column][/vc_row][/vc_section][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=16162 img_size=full css=][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] What the Science Says Research consistently shows that Curodont: Arrests or reverses early caries with high success rates Is more effective than fluoride varnish alone Works well for both adults and children Is excellent for orthodontic white spot lesions Has strong patient acceptance due to its comfort and ease For patients needing a white spot treatment in Dubai, Curodont has become one of the most recommended options.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] Suitable for: Early enamel decay (“white spots”) Early interproximal lesions on X-rays Orthodontic white spots after braces High caries-risk patients needing preventive strengthening Anyone looking for a non-invasive cavity treatment in the UAE[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] Not suitable for: Cavitated lesions (visible holes) Deep decay into dentin Painful or infected teeth Cases requiring biomimetic restorations or root canal therapy If Curodont isn’t appropriate, we will recommend the most conservative biomimetic restoration possible to preserve maximum tooth structure.

All-on-4: Key Similarities and Differences Both All-on-6 and All-on-4 implants are revolutionary techniques designed for full-arch restoration, offering immediate results and a natural-looking smile. However, there are key distinctions between the two: Similarities: Both procedures involve a fixed full-arch prosthesis supported by implants, eliminating the need for traditional removable dentures.

One of our premier treatments is the All-on-6 dental implant procedure, an innovative and long-lasting solution for patients with multiple missing teeth. What Are All-on-6 Dental Implants. The All-on-6 technique involves placing six titanium implants strategically into the jawbone to support a full arch of prosthetic teeth. This method provides exceptional stability, functionality, and aesthetics, making it an ideal option for those seeking a permanent alternative to dentures.

[/vc_toggle][vc_toggle title=How fast can I get started.

Stability and Distribution: With six implants, the weight of the prosthetic teeth is more evenly distributed, which can improve long-term success and comfort. Patients with lower bone density may be better suited for the All-on-6 method, as it distributes the force on 6 implants and often avoids the need for bone grafting. Durability and Chewing Power: All-on-6 implants offer greater chewing efficiency due to the additional support, making them ideal for patients with higher functional demands.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] Differences: Number of Implants: All-on-6 utilizes six implants, while All-on-4 uses four implants per arch. The additional two implants in the All-on-6 technique provide enhanced support and durability.

[vc_section][vc_row][vc_column width=1/2][vc_column_text css=] All-on-6 Dental Implants: A Permanent Solution for a Complete Smile At New Ivory Dental & Implant Clinic, we specialize in advanced dental solutions that restore your confidence and improve oral health.

The Procedure: Step by Step Comprehensive Consultation: Our experts perform a detailed assessment with 3D imaging to determine the best treatment approach. Implant Placement: Six implants are precisely positioned under local anesthesia, ensuring a secure foundation. Temporary Prosthesis: A functional temporary prosthetic is attached, so you can enjoy your smile right away. Healing Period: Over a few months, the implants integrate with the jawbone in a process called osseointegration.

Customization and Comfort: The All-on-6 method allows for better customization of the prosthetic teeth and enhanced natural feel. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=16146 img_size=full css=][/vc_column][/vc_row][/vc_section][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] Benefits of All-on-6 Implants Immediate Results: Walk out with a full set of functional teeth the same day.

Enhanced Stability: Six implants provide a stronger foundation compared to four, reducing the risk of long-term complications. Bone Preservation: Helps maintain jawbone integrity, preventing the sunken appearance that occurs with missing teeth. Cost-Effective: Compared to individual implants, this full-arch restoration is a more economical and efficient solution. Better Aesthetics and Functionality: Provides a more natural look, feel, and bite force, improving overall quality of life.

Broken File in Root Canal Treatment During root canal procedures, small dental instruments may be used to clean and shape the root canals. In some cases, an instrument fragment may separate within the canal due to factors such as canal anatomy, calcification, or instrument stress. The presence of a separated instrument may limit access to the canal system.

Indications for Root Canal Retreatment Root canal retreatment may be recommended in cases involving: Persistent or recurrent infection Untreated or previously unidentified canals Leakage or deterioration of existing restorations Structural or biological changes following prior treatment Treatment recommendations are based on individual clinical assessment.

Unlike traditional dental treatments that rely on invasive techniques and excessive drilling, biomimetic dentistry preserves as much of the original tooth as possible while providing strong, long-lasting restorations. What is Biomimetic Dentistry. Biomimetic dentistry is a modern, science-based approach that aims to restore teeth in a way that closely replicates their natural form, strength, and aesthetics.

By using advanced adhesive techniques and state-of-the-art materials, we can rebuild damaged teeth while maintaining their natural flexibility and durability. This prevents future fractures, sensitivity, and failures commonly associated with traditional restorations.

CEREC crowns are accurate to about 10 microns that is about a tenth of a human hair. They can also be done for same day implants to restore teeth that have been missing since years in just one appointment. No messy impressions, No temporary crowns and NO long waits.

CEREC has totally revolutionized restorative dentistry and how crowns, inlays and dental implant crowns that used to take a week to be done are now fabricated in the dental office in about 2-3 hours. It is the most advanced and cutting edge technology available today for crowns that not only are faster but also FIT better and LAST longer.

The entire workflow is digitised from the beginning using advanced 3D intra-oral scanners that take more than a thousand images a minute that are then reconstructed to a highly accurate 3 dimensional virtual model of the teeth and jaws. Planning of the crown is done by the dentist at the chairside. The artificial intelligence in the software interface proposes a crown design based on the shape of the existing teeth and suggests the best possible shapes and contours.

Scope of CBCT Dental Imaging: CBCT imaging may include visualization of: Teeth and tooth roots Jawbones Nerve canals Maxillary sinuses Temporomandibular joints (TMJ) The imaging field and exposure parameters are selected based on the clinical indication and referral requirements.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=1/2][vc_single_image image=6166 img_size=full][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=] What Is a CBCT Dental Imaging.

Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) is a radiographic imaging method used in dentistry to produce three-dimensional images of the teeth, jaws, and surrounding anatomical structures. The imaging captures hard tissues such as teeth and bone, as well as the spatial relationship between anatomical landmarks. CBCT imaging may be requested when two-dimensional dental radiographs are not sufficient for diagnostic evaluation.

Understanding Bone Loss After Tooth Loss When a tooth is lost, the root that once anchored it in place is also gone. This absence creates a void in the jawbone, leading to bone resorption over time. The underlying bone begins to diminish because it no longer receives the stimulation it needs from the tooth root to maintain its density. This loss of bone can have numerous effects on your oral health.

It can alter your facial structure, leading to a sunken appearance, and can even affect the alignment of your remaining teeth. Retraining the jawbone is crucial, and this is where dental implants in Dubai come into play. They provide a stable foundation for replacement teeth, effectively mimicking the function of natural tooth roots. The procedure is straightforward and typically completed in one visit. Root Canals : Involves removing infected or damaged pulp from inside the tooth. This procedure is more complex and may require multiple visits, depending on the severity of the infection.

Recovery Time Fillings : Generally, recovery is quick, with minimal discomfort. You can usually return to your normal activities immediately. Root Canals : Recovery may take longer, as the tooth and surrounding tissues need time to heal. Some discomfort is common after the procedure, but it can be managed with over-the-counter pain relief. Long-Term Outcomes Fillings : While fillings are effective for minor decay, they may need to be replaced over time as wear and tear occur.

Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) uses a cone-shaped X-ray beam to capture detailed three-dimensional images of the teeth, jawbone, and surrounding structures. Unlike traditional X-rays, CBCT provides a comprehensive view of the patients oral and maxillofacial region, allowing for a more accurate assessment and treatment planning. The CBCT machine rotates around the patients head, capturing multiple images that are then reconstructed into a single 3D image using specialized software.
